what_it_does: CSS for a channel-view overlay/modal that frames a graph visualization: overlay/panel/head/body/message styles, plus overrides that make the graph mount full-width, single-column, content-sized, with a definite SVG width.
observations: Uses CSS custom-property tokens throughout; no hardcoded colors except a 15px close-button font-size.
Overlay is a fixed-position flex centering surface with safe-area insets and a color-mix scrim.
Panel max-width is 760px, described as wider than session/decision modals.
Graph body is centered and the SVG is given a fixed 600px width capped at 100%.
Contains extensive inline comments explaining responsive overrides and prior bug behavior.
Overrides .screen.ar and .ar-responsive to remove a two-column grid and fixed device height.
Provides message variants --error and --quiet and a retry button style.
standouts: Dense narrative comments that document design decisions, bug history, and viewport test results.
Explicit override of an inherited .ar-responsive grid to a single-column flex layout.
Fixed 600px SVG width with max-width:100% to avoid collapse at wide viewports.
Safe-area padding integrated into overlay and body spacing.
conventions: BEM-like class naming with .channels-* block and -- modifiers.
Token-driven spacing, typography, color, radius, and elevation.
Flex column layout for the panel and graph mount.
44px minimum touch target on the close button.
concerns: Comments reference external files/components (DNA, phone.css, surface.css, hubNetwork) not defined here.
Close button uses a hardcoded font-size and line-height.
The .channels-graph .screen.ar override may conflict with other responsive rules in unseen files.
No visible token definitions or mapping within the file.
notes: This file is the CSS host frame for the channels overlay, centering a full-width graph visualization and overriding the inherited two-column responsive layout to prevent collapse or cropping.
questions: Where are the custom properties (--safe-t, --safe-b, --s-*, --t-*, --ink-*, --ground-*, --elev-4) defined?
What is the DNA fabric graph / hubNetwork component that this file frames?
What are .screen.ar and .ar-responsive in the files referenced by the comments?
How does this overlay relate structurally to the sessions/inbox overlays it mirrors?
What is the meaning of the --pig-strain token used for error messages?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it frames a channel-view overlay and graph mount, and adds responsive overrides to keep the graph visible.
apparent_themes: Separation of frame styling from graph content
intention_signals: Create a centered, accessible overlay that hosts a network graph and ensure the graph is not constrained by an inherited two-column phone-frame layout or fixed device height.
novelty: The file reads like a design log: comments narrate the discovery of graph cropping/collapse and the responsive override rationale, rather than only declaring styles.
contribution: A responsive overlay frame and graph-mount override that preserves a centered, full-width graph visualization across viewports by neutralizing inherited two-column and fixed-height constraints.
summary_for_embedding: CSS for the channels-view overlay and its graph mount, including panel, header, body, message, and retry styles plus overrides that force the graph's responsive frame to a single-column, content-sized, full-width layout with a 600px SVG.
intention_for_embedding: Provide a centered modal surface that hosts a fabric graph visualization without letting inherited two-column phone-frame responsive rules crop or collapse it.
